Event.description:
Live-Source Tax Certificate Forgery is a high-fidelity variant of business document falsification in which fraudsters generate counterfeit official tax certificates (for example, Mexico's Constancia de Situación Fiscal) populated in real time with authentic taxpayer data extracted from a compromised authoritative source (typically the issuing tax authority's own backend systems). Unlike traditional forgery that fabricates or alters a document's data fields, this technique relies on an active intrusion into the source-of-truth platform and a live query API that returns the genuine taxpayer record at the moment of certificate generation. The resulting PDF replicates the official visual format — letterhead, header, QR code, cadena original, digital seal placeholder — and binds it to live, accurate name, RFC/TIN, address, tax regime, economic activities, registration date, and fiscal obligations sourced directly from the breached platform.

Execution: Operators first establish persistent access to the tax authority's internal databases (via credential theft, web-shell deployment, privilege escalation, or AI-assisted exploitation of legacy enterprise software). They then build a thin REST/Flask API that pivots through their existing SSH/SOCKS proxy chain into the compromised environment, executes the same stored procedures, secondary database queries, SOAP enrichment lookups, and LDAP attribute reads that the legitimate certificate-issuance workflow uses, and assembles a single taxpayer profile from the live response. A companion PDF-generation script consumes that profile and renders a certificate that visually matches the official template. Because the data fields resolve correctly when manually checked against the legitimate registry, the documents pass routine non-cryptographic verification (visual inspection, address/RFC look-up, name match) even when the embedded digital seal itself is invalid or substituted.

Examples: Operating a 594-line Flask query API that on each request makes a stored-procedure call, an additional database query, a SOAP address-enrichment request, and an LDAP operational-attribute lookup against a compromised tax authority and merges the results into a taxpayer profile used by a downstream PDF generator; using a 541-line companion script to render a Constancia de Situación Fiscal PDF (header, QR code, cadena original, fabricated digital-seal field) populated with the live profile, and selling per-document or per-query access on underground channels; substituting the cryptographic seal with a fabricated marker while the underlying name, RFC, address, tax regime, economic activities, registration date, and fiscal obligations remain genuine.

Goals: Produce counterfeit tax-status, registration, or compliance certificates that survive routine, non-cryptographic verification because the underlying personal data matches the authoritative registry exactly; monetize sustained access to a compromised government or corporate source-of-truth system by selling per-document forgeries or per-query API subscriptions; enable downstream identity-bound fraud (loan applications, vendor onboarding, KYC submissions, tax refunds, benefits enrollment, immigration documentation) where the verifier's primary control is data cross-referencing rather than seal cryptography; preserve plausible deniability for buyers, who receive a document whose content is factually accurate even though its issuance is unauthorized.
Event.info: FT3 technique FT026.006: Falsifying Business Documents: Live-Source Tax Certificate Forgery
